#Latin singular "idae" was short for "disvidae" (apart + separate). Their original moon-cult calendar...
- Started with "calendar day"
- Then 4 or 6 days
- Then "nonae" ("9 before" ides)
- Then 8 days
- Then "idae" (divide day, full moon)
- Then an "a.d." countdown to the next new moon(-th).

images.alt:
A retro-styled magazine cover designed to resemble an educational publication from the early 1970s fills the frame. At the top, large bold lettering reads “Critical Developmental Learning,” set in warm brown serif type with a soft orange outline. Beneath it, the date “September 1972” appears, along with a price of seventy-five cents in the upper corner. The headline below the title reads “Stop, Drop, and Roll!” presented in large red lettering, evoking the tone of a safety lesson.

The cover is arranged like a collage of illustrated panels, each depicting children and an adult in bright, cheerful mid-century illustration style. In the upper left panel, a smiling blonde girl with two high pigtails walks while holding a large pair of scissors in her hand. She wears a small orange skirt, white shirt, and vest, and appears carefree as she moves across a simple pastel background. In the center top panel, a young boy stands waist-deep in a patch of quicksand with his arms raised in alarm, surrounded by sparse twig-like plants sticking out of the ground.

To the right, a portrait of a friendly adult woman with styled auburn hair smiles encouragingly, suggesting a teacher or guide. Below the panels, bold green text reads “Running with Scissors!” while another headline nearby reads “Strategies for Quicksand.”

Dominating the lower portion of the cover is a larger illustration of a young boy in an orange sweater and blue pants positioned on the grass on his hands and knees, demonstrating the posture associated with “stop, drop, and roll.” Nearby, an adult figure kneels partially in frame, while a small girl with dark braided hair watches with her hand covering her mouth in surprise.

Clearly identified as AI-generated; use is permitted for non-commercial creative projects. MidJourney, FLUX.1-schnell model via Perchance, Artspace.ai, REVE, Echoform™.

#FloralSanctuary #PeonyDream #AtmosphericArt #OrnateInterior #GenAI #SynthArt #AIart #aiartist #aiartcommunity #GenerativeArt #AIchallenge #BuilderofNightmares

images.alt:
At the center of an ornate interior chamber, an elderly man sits upright on a carved wooden platform that resembles a ceremonial throne. He wears a traditional dark robe with long sleeves and a fitted black cap, his posture composed and symmetrical, hands resting calmly on his knees. His expression is solemn and unreadable, giving the impression of quiet authority or ritual presence.

Behind him rises an elaborate wall of dark lacquered wood panels carved with intricate patterns. Framing this backdrop is an immense cascade of flowers, dominated by lush pink and crimson peonies. The blossoms form thick arches and clusters that spill from tall porcelain vases and decorative stands on either side of the throne, creating a living canopy of petals and foliage. Their textures range from tightly layered buds to fully open blooms, some drooping slightly with their weight.

In the foreground, more arrangements blur softly along the edges of the scene, forming a corridor of flowers that guide the eye toward the seated figure. Petals are scattered across the polished stone floor, which reflects the colors of the blossoms and the carved furniture like a dark mirror. The light is subdued and cinematic, filtering gently from above and the sides so that the flowers glow against the shadowed architecture.

The overall atmosphere feels ceremonial and contemplative. The abundance of blossoms contrasts with the stillness of the lone figure, as though the room itself has bloomed into a temporary shrine built from fragrance, color, and quiet time.

Keywords
peonies, floral throne, traditional interior, ceremonial portrait, ornate wood carvings, pink blossoms, reflective floor, atmospheric stillness, cinematic composition, imperial aesthetic
